sources.title: Methods in causal inference. Part 1: causal diagrams and confounding
sources.publisher: Cambridge University Press / Evolutionary Human Sciences
sources.locator: Abstract; causal-inference assumptions; causal-diagram conventions; confounding, reverse-causation, mediator, and collider examples; conclusion

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used only to verify causal contrasts, explicit identifying assumptions, common-cause confounding, reverse direction, mediator and collider structures, and limits of adjustment. No wording, example, table, figure, or source organization is reused.

agentAccess.notes: Before substantive access, the NCBI policies and robots route were checked. The generic PMC article route was not used because that robots route disallows it; the public NCBI BioC REST endpoint for PMC11588567, which is not disallowed, and PubMed metadata were used without login, paywall, rate-limit breach, or access-control bypass. No agent-ingestion prohibition was found for the route used.

sources.license: PMC text-mining copy; no open-expression licence displayed

sources.title: The use of mechanistic evidence in drug approval

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: Used only to verify that mechanistic evidence concerns the existence or details of a process connecting a proposed cause and effect and can complement outcome studies and generalization judgments. sources.title: Triangulation in aetiological epidemiology
